definitions.text: To perceive the truth or factuality of; to be certain of; to be certain that.
examples: I know that I’m right and you’re wrong.
He knew something terrible was going to happen.

definitions.text: To be or become aware or cognizant.
examples: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.
Malware's sometimes been known to sit dormant for a long time.

definitions.text: To be aware of; to be cognizant of.
examples: Did you know Michelle and Jack were getting divorced? ― Yes, I knew.

definitions.text: Knowledge; the state of knowing. (Now confined to the fixed phrase in the know.)
Used at the end of a sentence to draw attention to information one thinks the listener should keep in mind.
examples: Make sure you water the plants, know…
I was a naval diver know!
